definition: Notebook best practices are the habits that make notebooks reproducible, reviewable and maintainable: ordered idempotent cells, restart-and-run-all verification, extracting reusable logic into modules, stripping outputs for version control, and keeping secrets and large data out of the file.
layman: These are the rules that stop notebooks from becoming messy, unreproducible 'works only on my machine' files — and help you graduate good ideas into real, reusable code.
content:
## Notebook best practices

Notebooks are fantastic for exploration and dangerous for production if left undisciplined. A few habits fix most problems.

### 1. Make it reproducible

Develop top-to-bottom and **Restart & Run All** before trusting any result. Cells should run cleanly in order from a fresh kernel — no reliance on hidden state.

### 2. Keep cells small and ordered

One idea per cell, arranged so the notebook reads like a story. Avoid giant cells and avoid depending on cells you've since edited or deleted.

### 3. Graduate logic into modules

When code stabilizes, move it from cells into a `.py` module and `import` it. Modules are testable, reusable, version-control-friendly, and keep the notebook focused on narrative and results.

### 4. Treat notebooks kindly in version control

Notebooks are JSON with embedded outputs, so they diff and merge badly. **Strip outputs before committing** (e.g. `nbstripout`), and consider `jupytext` to pair a notebook with a plain `.py` for clean diffs, or `nbdime` for notebook-aware diffs.

### 5. Protect secrets and data

Never hard-code API keys or paste large/PII outputs into a notebook — they get saved and shared. Read keys from the environment (or Colab Secrets) and keep big data out of the file.

### Why this matters for Gen AI

Gen AI work lives in notebooks: prompt experiments, eval runs, embedding inspection. These habits make experiments reproducible by teammates, keep model responses and keys out of committed files, and let proven prompt/eval logic move cleanly into a real package or pipeline.
keyPoints: Restart & Run All to guarantee a clean, ordered, reproducible run.
Small, single-purpose, ordered cells; no reliance on hidden state.
Extract stable logic into importable .py modules for testing and reuse.
Strip outputs before committing; use jupytext/nbdime for sane diffs.
Keep API keys and large/PII data out of the notebook file.
